name: DESMOPLASTIC SMALL ROUND CELL TUMOR
definition: INTRAABDOMINAL DESMOPLASTIC SMALL ROUND CELL TUMOR OF PERITONEUM rare highly malignant tumor belonging to a generic group of small round blue cell tumors (like

demographics.Path: multinodular unencapsulated mass with focal cystic areas / necrosis / hemorrhage;
demographics.Histo: island clusters of small blue cells embedded in a dense desmoplastic fibrous stroma;
demographics.Genetics: chromosomal translocation t(11;22)(p13;q12) creating a unique fusion protein of

dominant_finding: larger (> 10 cm) mass with central area of low attenuation + heterogeneous enhancement ←
distribution: diffuse nodular peritoneal thickening + omental caking
discriminating_features: multiple scattered necrotic peritoneal, omental, and serosal round / ovoid masses without a clear
usually single dominant (> 10 cm) mass in the retrovesical / retrouterine space / peritoneum /
diffuse nodular peritoneal thickening + omental caking
tumor spread to serosal surface of liver + spleen (common)

general: multiple scattered necrotic peritoneal, omental, and serosal round / ovoid masses without a clear
usually single dominant (> 10 cm) mass in the retrovesical / retrouterine space / peritoneum /
diffuse nodular peritoneal thickening + omental caking
tumor spread to serosal surface of liver + spleen (common)

US: hyper- / hypoechoic mass ← depending on degree of necrosis + hemorrhage
CT: small masses of low attenuation (24-36 HU) + uniform enhancement
larger (> 10 cm) mass with central area of low attenuation + heterogeneous enhancement ←
hypoattenuating metastases to liver, abdominal and retroperitoneal lymph nodes
MR: mass typically hypo- to isointense on T1WI + heterogeneously iso- to hyperintense on
occasional areas of T2-hypointensity ← densely cellular components / dense desmoplastic
nonenhancing central foci ← hemorrhage / necrosis / fibromyxoid component
